entry.text: She is sitting against a collapsed wall, holding her ankle, and she looks genuinely hurt. Her voice carries the right amount of distress — not too much, not too little. 'Please,' she says, 'I think it's broken. I can't walk.' You get within five metres and something stops you — an instinct, a smell, the way her eyes track too precisely for someone focused on pain. You hesitate. She sees you hesitate. Her expression changes in a way that bypasses all social context and goes straight to something much older and considerably worse.

choices.label: Step back fast and keep moving
Use the wall to your advantage and fight

escaped_cleanly.text: You keep the wall between you and her and move fast without running. The sound behind you is not a person in distress — it is something considerably more motivated. But the gap widens. You don't stop until you've put three blocks between you and whatever that was. When you finally allow yourself to breathe, your hands are shaking.

attacker_down.text: It's over quickly once you stop holding back. The loot around the nest site suggests this trap has been running for a while — multiple victims, multiple rounds of picked-over supplies. You collect what's salvageable without looking too hard at context. A good haul. Bad feelings.

bite_caught.text: The jaw unhinges faster than you saw it coming. You get your forearm up and the teeth close on that instead of somewhere worse. The grip is enormous and the bite goes deep before you can break free.

bite_caught_bad.text: She gets a bite in before you can close the distance. The teeth are real, the jaw strength is significant, and the wound is deep. You finish the fight but you finish it worse.
